chief_complaint: An 8-year-old castrated male Miniature Poodle presented with polyuria, polydipsia, and urinary incontinence.
history: The dog was referred after 12 days of prednisolone administration (0.25 mg/kg q12h) for otitis externa. There is no other past medical history mentioned.
exam: An abnormal heart rhythm was detected on cardiac auscultation without any associated clinical signs.
labs: ECG revealed a Mobitz Type II second-degree atrioventricular (AV) block. Blood analysis showed hyperglycaemia (> 500 mg/dL), elevated fructosamine (361 micromol/L), and beta-hydroxybutyric acid concentrations (5.4 mmol/L).
correct_diagnosis: Diabetic ketoacidosis with second-degree atrioventricular block
differentials: Diabetic ketoacidosis with second-degree atrioventricular block
correct_ranking: Diabetic ketoacidosis with second-degree atrioventricular block
